title: ophrys cornuta var. dodekanensis
intro: Ophrys cornuta var. dodekanensis, commonly known as the Dodecanese bee orchid, is a rare and endemic orchid species found in the Dodecanese Islands of Greece. This plant is valued for its potential medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. The therapeutic actions of Ophrys cornuta var. dodekanensis are thought to be attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and cytotoxic activities.
